boundaries.p267c_conclusion_boundary: P267C (M3+ revalidation) found NO_VALIDATED_M3_EDGE across 36/36 cells for the existing replay-backed strategy family. This does NOT close the broader success-rate / hit-rate research line.
boundaries.p268b_conclusion_boundary: P268B (PR #407, merged into main @ f3c933b) is a diagnostics-only prototype. Bounded sample (2026-04/2026-05, 5 games, 190 records). drawNumberAppear field structurally valid: main-pool records 154/154 present/correct-length/sorted-equal-to-drawNumberSize; 3_STAR/4_STAR 52/52 each present and correct length; 0 parse failures. DB alignment = NO_LOCAL_ROWS (schema-level only, read-only mode=ro). Positional diagnostics (chi2-vs-uniform, adjacent-diff rates) are descriptive only, no p-value gate, NO hit-rate or success-rate claim made.
boundaries.track_b_isolation: winnerCount / prize-distribution / EV (Track B, P268A Direction B) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this hit-rate design line and must not be merged into H1/H2/H3 or any P268D plan.

causal_chain.description: drawNumberAppear positional bias does NOT directly equal a hit-rate edge. It must pass through the following causal chain before any strategy claim is admissible.

steps.name: order_bias_detection
steps.description: Within-draw permutation test detects per-ball mean exit-rank heterogeneity (i.e., some balls systematically drawn 'earlier' or 'later' than a fully exchangeable null predicts).

steps.name: earliness_score_construction
steps.description: If step 1 passes, derive a per-ball 'earliness score' from an estimation window only (e.g., standardized mean exit-rank conditional on inclusion).

steps.name: oos_inclusion_prediction
steps.description: Test whether the earliness score, computed strictly from the estimation window, predicts ball-inclusion-frequency deviations in a disjoint holdout window (single pre-registered direction, not a scan).

steps.name: candidate_hit_rate_strategy
steps.description: Only if step 3 passes (and stability in H3 holds) does this become a CANDIDATE for a hit-rate strategy design task — which would be a SEPARATE, future, explicitly-authorized task subject to P221F, McNemar (L48), and full three-window validation. No strategy is designed in P268C or P268D.

diagnostics_only_conditions: Pure draw-order effect with uniform inclusion: if exit-rank heterogeneity exists (step 1 passes) but inclusion-frequency distribution remains statistically indistinguishable from uniform, the finding is diagnostics-only — order bias does not transfer to which numbers are drawn.

Insufficient effect size given sample depth: BIG_LOTTO (~2,100 draws) and POWER_LOTTO/DAILY_539 depths are far below the minimum-detectable-effect thresholds established in L91 (+0.789% minimum detectable margin at power=0.80); any observed effect within this band is diagnostics-only.

Non-stationarity across ball-set/machine eras: P268A confirmed the public API exposes NO machine/ball-set ID field, so era segmentation is not possible from this data source; if true bias is era-specific and eras cannot be isolated, any full-history pooled signal averages toward null and is diagnostics-only.

Field-semantics risk: if drawNumberAppear reflects broadcast/presentation order rather than true physical draw order, the entire causal chain (steps 1-4) is invalid regardless of statistical results; P268D must include a semantics-verification note (out of scope to resolve fully without video review, flagged as a limitation).

Multiple-comparisons collapse: with 5 games x multiple positions x multiple balls, any uncorrected finding is diagnostics-only until it survives the pre-registered Bonferroni / single-primary-game design below.

full_history_api_feasibility.query_pattern: GET {endpoint}?period&month=YYYY-MM&pageNum=1&pageSize=31, no auth, verified live back to 2007-01 (P268A/P268B)
full_history_api_feasibility.estimated_call_volume: ~12 months/year x ~19 years (2007-2026) x 5 games = ~1,140 monthly calls for full backfill; must be rate-limited (no burst), resumable, and bounded per run (e.g., a fixed monthly call budget per session) -- no single unbounded loop.
full_history_api_feasibility.transport_note: P268B found Python urllib fails TLS verification against this host (Missing Subject Key Identifier) while curl succeeds via system CA store; P268D fetcher must use the curl-subprocess approach already validated in analysis/p268b_official_draw_order_positional_bias_audit.py.
full_history_api_feasibility.constraint: P268D itself does NOT execute this fetch. It only documents the plan. Execution requires a separate explicitly-authorized task.

artifact_store_backfill_plan.storage: outputs/research/ (JSONL or per-game CSV/JSON artifacts), append-only, NOT data/lottery_v2.db
artifact_store_backfill_plan.checkpoint_ledger: A resumable ingest ledger (month x game completion flags) so a future bounded run can resume without re-fetching completed months.
artifact_store_backfill_plan.rate_limiting: Sequential, single in-flight request, explicit per-run call cap; PARTIAL status on any endpoint instability rather than retry storms (per P268B precedent).

db_alignment_plan.current_limitation: P268B found local data/lottery_v2.db `draws` table has 0 rows in this checkout (NO_LOCAL_ROWS). Row-level alignment between drawNumberAppear (period+lottery_type) and the canonical draws table could not be exercised.
db_alignment_plan.plan: Any future alignment check must (a) confirm which DB file/path is the canonical populated instance (not assume the checkout-local empty file), (b) open it strictly read-only (mode=ro), (c) match on (lottery_type, draw=period) join key already used elsewhere in this codebase, (d) report match-rate as a data-quality gate input.
db_alignment_plan.future_db_write_scope: If H1 (and ultimately H2) pass and a strategy design task is separately authorized, a NEW column or side-table (e.g., draw_order_sequence) would be the eventual DB-write candidate -- explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for P268C and P268D, listed only as a later-phase placeholder requiring its own authorization.

hypothesis_registry_draft.note: DRAFT ONLY. Not written to lottery_api/data/hypothesis_registry.jsonl. A future P268D-or-later task would formally register these entries before any full-history fetch begins.
hypothesis_registry_draft.primary_game_rationale: Largest available draw depth (~5,880 draws), maximizing statistical power for the within-draw permutation test; BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO are treated as secondary/co-primary at governance discretion.

hypotheses.name: per_ball_exit_rank_heterogeneity
hypotheses.statement: Per-ball mean exit-rank (position within drawNumberAppear, conditional on the ball being drawn) is heterogeneous across balls, beyond what a within-draw fully-exchangeable permutation null predicts.
hypotheses.null_model: Within-draw permutation null: condition on the observed drawn set per draw; all orderings of that set are equally likely. Generate >=10,000 permutation replicates per game.
hypotheses.test_statistic: Dispersion (e.g., variance or chi2-style statistic) of standardized per-ball mean exit-rank across all balls in the pool.
hypotheses.window: Estimation window only (see windows.estimation_window below); 2026-04 and 2026-05 EXCLUDED from any confirmatory test (already inspected in P268B).
hypotheses.correction: Bonferroni across games tested (denominator = number of games included in this round; if DAILY_539-only design is chosen, no cross-game correction needed for H1 itself).
hypotheses.pass_criteria: Estimation-window corrected permutation p < 0.01 AND the same direction/effect replicates with holdout-window p < 0.05 (see H1_holdout below).
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Estimation-window corrected p >= 0.01, OR holdout replication fails -> classify as NULL / diagnostics-only, permanent closure artifact, H2 NOT opened.

hypotheses.name: exit_rank_heterogeneity_holdout_replication
hypotheses.statement: The per-ball exit-rank heterogeneity pattern identified in the H1 estimation window replicates (same direction, p<0.05) in the disjoint holdout window.
hypotheses.gating: Only computed if H1 estimation-window result passes its threshold. Holdout window is sealed/unopened until then.

hypotheses.name: earliness_score_predicts_inclusion_frequency
hypotheses.statement: A per-ball 'earliness score' derived strictly from the estimation window predicts the direction of inclusion-frequency deviation (vs uniform 6/49-or-equivalent baseline) in the holdout window.
hypotheses.null_model: Inclusion frequency under uniform random draw (closed-form per game, e.g., C(n-1,k-1)/C(n,k) per ball per draw).
hypotheses.test_design: Single pre-registered one-tailed test of correlation (e.g., Spearman) between estimation-window earliness score and holdout-window inclusion-frequency deviation. NOT a scan over alternative score definitions.
hypotheses.correction: None required if pre-registered as a single test; any deviation from the pre-registered score definition voids the test.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: One-tailed p < 0.05 in the pre-registered direction.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: p >= 0.05 or wrong-direction -> diagnostics-only closure; this does NOT reopen H1.

hypotheses.name: earliness_score_temporal_stability
hypotheses.statement: The per-ball earliness score is stable across a split-half (or era-split, if any segmentation becomes possible) of the estimation window.
hypotheses.test_design: Spearman rank correlation of earliness scores between the two halves.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Non-significant or negative correlation -> treat H1/H2 results as fragile / non-stable, do not proceed to strategy design even if H1/H2 nominally pass.
hypotheses.gating: Computed alongside H1_holdout; informs robustness assessment, not a hard gate on its own but a required input to any future go/no-go.

windows.full_history_range: 2007-01 to most recent available month, EXCLUDING 2026-04 and 2026-05 (already inspected by P268B prototype -- excluded from confirmatory analysis to avoid look-ahead contamination)
windows.split: 70% estimation / 30% holdout, split chronologically (estimation = earliest 70% of eligible draws, holdout = most recent 30% of eligible draws, both excluding 2026-04/05)
windows.split_half_for_h3: Estimation window further split chronologically in half for H3.

baseline.h1_baseline: Within-draw permutation null (exchangeable ordering given the drawn set).
baseline.h2_baseline: Closed-form uniform inclusion probability per ball given pool size and draw size.

p221f_p_hacking_guardrails: All hypotheses, windows, baselines, and pass/fail thresholds frozen BEFORE any full-history data is fetched (registry-freeze precedes backfill, per P268D step 1).
2026-04/05 permanently excluded from confirmatory tests (P268B already inspected this window).
Holdout window sealed/not computed until H1 estimation-window result is finalized.
H2 score definition fixed at registration time; any later change to the score definition requires a new hypothesis ID (no silent redefinition).
No scan over alternative null models, statistics, or windows after seeing results; any such exploration must be logged as a separate, clearly-labeled exploratory (non-confirmatory) note.
Single primary game (DAILY_539) for H1 minimizes multiple-testing burden; if co-primary games are added, Bonferroni applies across the co-primary set.
Track B (winnerCount/EV) variables must not be added to H1/H2/H3 feature sets.

p268d_implementation_order.ranking_rationale: registry/pre-registration FIRST (no data-driven hypothesis tuning is possible), THEN full-history artifact backfill (data must exist before alignment or testing), THEN alignment/data-quality (must trust data before testing), THEN H1 confirmatory test LAST.

steps.name: freeze_registry_artifact
steps.description: Formally register H1/H1_holdout/H2/H3 (from this draft) into the Hypothesis Registry, with windows/baseline/correction/pass-fail frozen, BEFORE any full-history fetch begins.

steps.name: bounded_rate_full_history_fetcher
steps.description: Implement a curl-subprocess-based fetcher (per P268B precedent) writing per-month per-game results to outputs/research/ JSONL/CSV artifacts. No DB write.

steps.name: checkpoint_resume_ledger
steps.description: Maintain a (month x lottery_type) completion ledger so runs are resumable and bounded; PARTIAL status on instability, no aggressive retries.

steps.name: structure_validation
steps.description: Per P268B's checks: drawNumberAppear presence, correct length, sorted-equivalence to drawNumberSize, applied to every backfilled record; failures logged.

steps.name: annual_zip_csv_cross_check_sample
steps.description: Cross-validate a bounded sample against the official annual ZIP/CSV archive (Direction D) to catch transcription drift.

steps.name: readonly_canonical_db_alignment
steps.description: Identify the canonical populated DB instance, open read-only (mode=ro), and compute (lottery_type, draw=period) match-rate against the backfilled artifact set.

steps.name: data_quality_gate
steps.description: Define and apply a coverage/structure-failure-rate gate (e.g., >=99% coverage, <0.1% structure failures); FAIL halts progression to step 8.

steps.name: h1_estimation_window_test
steps.description: Run H1 (within-draw permutation test) on the estimation window only, per the frozen registry; holdout remains sealed.

steps.name: h1_pass_or_fail_branch
steps.description: If H1 passes its threshold: open holdout for H1_holdout + H3. If H1 fails: produce a diagnostics-only / NULL closure artifact; H2 is NOT opened; do not reopen via re-scan.

steps.name: governance_closeout
steps.description: Record final classification (PASS-chain-continues vs NULL-closure) in CURRENT_STATE.md / active_task.md. No hit-rate or success-rate-improvement claim is made in this artifact regardless of outcome; any strategy-design follow-up requires a separate, future, explicitly-authorized task.

explicit_non_claims: No claim that hit-rate / success-rate has been improved.
No production DB write performed or authorized by this artifact.
No Hypothesis Registry write performed (draft only).
Track B (winnerCount/EV/popularity) is not part of this hit-rate design line.
